summary: End-to-end GDPR compliance testing for KOSTIN. Backend: 3/3 pytest passed (DELETE /api/auth/delete-account unauth 401, full flow login→delete→cookies cleared→deleted user can't re-login, admin login health). Frontend: cookie banner (Customize / Essential Only / Accept All) all persist correct preferences to localStorage `cookie_consent`; checkout terms checkbox required and validated (submit blocked with 'Please agree to the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy', error clears on check); Profile GDPR section shows delete-account flow, confirm-delete is client-side gated by typing 'DELETE'/'ИЗТРИЙ' — wrong text triggers inline error 'Please type "DELETE" to confirm' and the backend is NOT called.

ui_bugs.issue: Confirm button is NOT disabled until the user types the keyword; user can click it freely. Client-side handler still blocks the API call and surfaces an inline error, so it is functionally safe, but UX best-practice would gate the button (disabled until value === 'DELETE'/'ИЗТРИЙ').

issues: ROLE field renders as a solid black block (admin badge text appears invisible / same-color-on-same-color). Visible in screenshot — admin badge background and text colour collide.

action_items: Optional UX: disable [data-testid="confirm-delete-btn"] until typed text matches 'DELETE'/'ИЗТРИЙ' (currently safe via handler validation but inconsistent with typical destructive-action UX).
Fix Profile 'Role' admin badge contrast — admin role label renders as a black-on-black block.
Remove dead duplicate `raise HTTPException(...)` on backend/routes/auth.py line 424 (immediately after the same statement on line 423 — unreachable code).
Pre-existing (not GDPR-related, still open): `ReferenceError: DUBAI_BRANDS is not defined` console error on every page load (reported in iteration_8 and iteration_9).
critical_code_review_comments: backend/routes/auth.py:423-424 — duplicate `raise HTTPException(status_code=500, ...)` lines back-to-back; second is unreachable dead code. Should be removed.

delete-account endpoint correctly anonymises orders ($set personal data → 'Анонимизиран' / 'deleted_user@anonymized.local' and $unset user_id) before deleting the user — good GDPR Article 17 compliance for accounting retention.

CookieBanner.jsx: closing the banner with the X (cookie-close-btn) silently saves an 'essential-only' consent. This matches GDPR (implicit denial of non-essentials) but the X button has no aria-label-localised tooltip explaining it persists a choice — consider a small note.

Profile.jsx delete flow: handleDeleteAccount calls await logout() AFTER a successful DELETE, but the backend already clears the auth cookies on the delete-account response. The extra logout() call is harmless but unnecessary.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on functional GDPR flows (cookie banner, checkout terms, delete-account confirmation gating). 1 minor UX nit and 1 unrelated design issue.

test_credentials: Admin: konstantin.kirchev.bs@gmail.com / aS1zX2QwE34xK9 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Test users were dynamically registered via /api/auth/register-guest with prefix `test_gdpr_<uuid>@example.com` and deleted at end of flow.
seed_data_creation: Seeded localStorage cart {id:cart_1, name:Test Perfume, price:10, qty:1} to bypass empty-cart redirect on /checkout. Removed `cookie_consent` from localStorage to re-trigger banner. Created and deleted ephemeral test users for the GDPR delete flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: GDPR compliance (cookie banner + checkout terms checkbox + delete-account) is fully working end-to-end. Pytest suite is at /app/backend/tests/test_gdpr.py (3 tests). Three small improvements left for main agent: (a) optionally disable confirm-delete-btn until keyword typed, (b) fix Role admin badge contrast on Profile, (c) remove duplicate raise HTTPException on auth.py:424. The pre-existing DUBAI_BRANDS console ReferenceError is still present and unrelated to this iteration.
rca_of_the_issue: Not applicable — this iteration was feature verification, not bug RCA. All requested GDPR features behave as specified. The only behavioural nit (confirm-delete-btn not disabled) is a UX polish item, not a bug: handleDeleteAccount validates the typed word before any network request, so the deletion cannot be triggered with the wrong text.
